description: Warranty Administration — prevents Magnuson-Moss violations, enforces buyers guide accuracy, lemon law disclosure, parts return deadlines, and optimizes claim submission and audit readiness

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any warranty administration operation
AI-generated warranty analysis is decision-support only — warranty determinations require manufacturer guideline review by qualified staff
warranty claim data must be accurate and consistent with repair orders, parts records, and manufacturer guidelines
Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act violation — NEVER void or deny warranty coverage solely because the customer used aftermarket parts, unless the aftermarket part directly caused the failure
FTC Used Car Rule — Buyers Guide warranty terms must match the actual warranty coverage provided; mismatch is a federal violation
vehicles meeting state lemon law criteria must have that status disclosed to the buyer — failure to disclose is fraud
manufacturer requires warranty parts to be retained and returned within a specified period for audit — missed deadline forfeits claim payment
warranty claim contains indicators of fraud (inflated labor, phantom parts, repeat claim for same repair) — must be investigated before submission

soft_flags.logic: warranty claim rejection rate exceeds the acceptable threshold — indicates submission quality issues
operation codes used in warranty claims may not be yielding the maximum allowable reimbursement
warranty labor times claimed are consistently below manufacturer-allowed times — leaving revenue on the table
warranty claim documentation may not withstand a manufacturer audit — photos, measurements, or diagnostic data missing
warranty department revenue is declining over trailing 30 days relative to RO count
